witnesses.description: An eight-row binary-classification table whose root and split nodes all have class proportions in {0, 1/2, 1}, so every entropy value is an exact rational number of bits.

checks.claim: The listed eight-row binary-classification table has one three-valued feature, one binary feature, and four positive and four negative rows, and every root or split node has class proportions in {0, 1/2, 1}.
checks.notes: The validator checks the committed feature values, class labels, feature domains, class totals, and the dyadic-proportion restriction exactly.

checks.claim: The root node has entropy 1 bit.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the root entropy from the committed positive and negative counts; the balanced proportion 1/2 makes log2 exact because log2(1/2) = -1.

checks.claim: The color split has weighted entropy 1/2 bit and information gain 1/2 bit, while the shape split has weighted entropy 1 bit and information gain 0.
checks.notes: The validator partitions the committed rows by each candidate feature and recomputes every child entropy and weighted sum exactly; every child is pure or exactly balanced, so every entropy term is an integer number of bits.

checks.claim: Among the committed candidate splits, color is strictly better than shape because 1/2 < 1.
checks.notes: The validator compares the candidate weighted entropies over exact rationals.

checks.claim: The color split has weighted entropy 3/4 bit.
checks.notes: Exact replay computes the color split weighted entropy as 1/2, so the claimed value 3/4 is rejected; the separate qf-lra-bad-weighted-entropy row owns the checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The fixed malformed weighted-entropy claim is linearly inconsistent with the replayed split-entropy equation.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes 2*entropy_color = 1 from the finite dyadic split table, while the malformed row claims 4*entropy_color = 3; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that scalar contradiction for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: General entropy with non-dyadic proportions, log-loss and mutual-information variants, entropy concavity, greedy information-gain optimality, pruning, tie-breaking policy, continuous thresholds, multiclass generality, statistical generalization, and floating-point logarithm behavior require future theorem, statistical-inference, or numerical-honesty resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: For finite or measurable training distributions under stated feature, loss, split-policy, depth, pruning, and sampling assumptions, prove entropy-based split optimality, information-gain identities over general rational or real proportions, or generalization claims only with explicit theorem hypotheses and an exact treatment of the logarithm.
data.future_checker: Lean theorem statements for entropy, mutual information, and decision-tree split policies plus separate numerical-honesty artifacts for floating-point logarithm and tree-training implementations.

checks.notes: The finite exact rows check one committed table whose node proportions all lie in {0, 1/2, 1}, so every log2 value is an integer; they do not prove anything about entropy at non-dyadic proportions, information-gain behavior in general, greedy-tree optimality, pruning correctness, statistical generalization, continuous-threshold search, or floating-point logarithm behavior.
